chapter_title.en: Chapter on Currency Exchange and Its Forms

text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلدَّرَاهِمُ بِالدَّرَاهِمِ فِي إِحْدَاهُمَا رَصَاصٌ وَزْناً بِوَزْنٍ قَالَ أَعِدْ فَأَعَدْتُ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَعِدْ فَأَعَدْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "لاَ أَرَى بِهِ بَأْساً".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4042 - It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about exchanging dirhams for dirhams, where one of them contains lead, but they are equal in weight.
Imam (as) said: "Repeat the question."
So I repeated it to him.
Then Imam (as) said: "Repeat it again."
I repeated it again, and Imam (as) said: "I see no harm in it."
